title: Nvidia Will Spend $26 Billion to Build Open-Weight AI Models, Filings Show
summary: The move could position the AI infrastructure powerhouse to quickly compete with OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek.
title_es: NVIDIA invertirá $26 mil millones en modelos IA de código abierto
bajada: Según documentos de SEC, NVIDIA planea invertir $26 mil millones en el desarrollo de modelos IA de peso abierto para competir con OpenAI, Anthropic y DeepSeek. El movimiento posiciona al gigante de infraestructura como generador de modelos además de proveedora de hardware.
bajada_en: According to SEC filings, NVIDIA plans to invest $26 billion in developing open-weight AI models to compete with OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. The move positions the infrastructure giant as a model creator alongside hardware provider.

title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
summary: How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable agents with files, tools, and state.
title_es: OpenAI lanza agentes con API de Respuestas y soporte para contenedores
bajada: OpenAI presentó una nueva arquitectura para agentes que combina la API de Respuestas, herramientas de shell y contenedores alojados para ejecutar sistemas de IA autónomos seguros y escalables. Esta actualización permite que los desarrolladores construyan agentes más robustos con manejo de archivos, herramientas integradas y estado persistente.
bajada_en: OpenAI introduced a new agent architecture combining the Responses API, shell tools, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable autonomous AI systems. This update enables developers to build more robust agents with file handling, integrated tools, and persistent state management.

title: New NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super Delivers 5x Higher Throughput for Agentic AI

El modelo combina capacidades avanzadas de razonamiento y está disponible inmediatamente, con Perplexity como early adopter.
bajada_en: NVIDIA unveiled Nemotron 3 Super, an open-source 120-billion-parameter model with 12 billion active parameters designed to run complex agentic AI systems at scale. The model combines advanced reasoning capabilities and is available now, with Perplexity as an early adopter.

bajada_en: Zendesk completed the acquisition of Forethought, a pioneering agentic AI startup for customer service and TechCrunch Battlefield 2018 winner. The acquisition strengthens Zendesk's strategy to automate support services with agentic AI.

bajada_en: Perplexity introduced Personal Computer, a tool that transforms a spare Mac into an AI agent running 24/7 on your local network. Positioned as a "digital proxy for you," it enables autonomous task delegation without cloud dependency.

La feature proporciona respuestas detalladas y personalizadas, mejorando significativamente la usabilidad frente a búsquedas tradicionales.

title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection

bajada_en: OpenAI published detailed analysis on how ChatGPT defends against prompt injection and social engineering in agent workflows. The document explores techniques for restricting risky actions and protecting sensitive data, critical for production agents.

title: I was backend lead at Manus. After building agents for 2 years, I stopped using function calling entirely. Here's what I use instead.
summary: English is not my first language. I wrote this in Chinese and translated it with AI help. The writing may have some AI flavor, but the design decisions, the production failures, and the thinking that distilled them into principles — those are mine.I was a backend lead at Manus before the Meta acquisition. I've spent the last 2 years building AI agents — first at Manus, then on my own open-source agent runtime (Pinix) and agent (agent-clip). Along the way I came to a conclusion that surprised me:A singlerun(command="...")tool with Unix-style commands outperforms a catalog of typed function calls.Here's what I learned.Why *nixUnix made a design decision 50 years ago:everything is a text stream.Programs don't exchange complex binary structures or share memory objects — they communicate through text pipes. Small tools each do one thing well, composed via|into powerful workflows. Programs describe themselves with--help, report success or failure with exit codes, and communicate errors through stderr.LLMs made an almost identical decision 50 years later:everything is tokens.They only understand text, only produce text. Their "thinking" is text, their "actions" are text, and the feedback they receive from the world must be text.These two decisions, made half a century apart from completely different starting points, converge on the same interface model. The text-based system Unix designed for human terminal operators —cat,grep,pipe,exit codes,man pages— isn't just "usable" by LLMs. It's anatural fit. When it comes to tool use, an LLM is essentially a terminal operator — one that's faster than any human and has already seen vast amounts of shell commands and CLI patterns in its training data.This is the core philosophy of thenix Agent: *don't invent a new tool interface. Take what Unix has proven over 50 years and hand it directly to the LLM.**Why a singlerunThe single-tool hypothesisMost agent frameworks give LLMs a catalog of independent tools:tools: [search_web, read_fil
title_es: Ex-ingeniero de Manus: function calling es innecesario en agentes IA
bajada: Un ingeniero backend que lideró el desarrollo de agentes en Manus (adquirida por Meta) compartió en Reddit un análisis sobre por qué abandonó function calling a favor de comandos Unix en texto plano. El post generó debate significativo sobre los mejores patrones para construir agentes robustos.
bajada_en: A backend engineer who led agent development at Manus (acquired by Meta) shared on Reddit why he abandoned function calling in favor of plain-text Unix commands. The post sparked significant debate on best patterns for building robust agents.

title: Meta announces four new MTIA chips, focussed on inference
summary: Meta shared details on four generations of their custom MTIA chips (300–500), all developed in roughly two years.Meta's building their own silicon and iterating fast, a new chip roughly every 6 months, using modular chiplets where they can swap out pieces without redesigning everything.Notable:Inference-first design. MTIA 450 and 500 are optimized for GenAI inference, not training. Opposite of how Nvidia does it (build for training, apply to everything). Makes sense given their scale.HBM bandwidth scaling hard. 6.1 TB/s on the 300 → 27.6 TB/s on the 500 (4.5x). Memory bandwidth is the LLM inference bottleneck, and they claim MTIA 450 already beats leading commercial products here.Heavy low-precision push. MX4 hits 30 PFLOPS on the 500. Custom data types designed for inference that they say preserve model quality while boosting throughput.PyTorch-native with vLLM support. torch.compile, Triton, vLLM plugin. Models run on both GPUs and MTIA without rewrites.Timeline: MTIA 400 heading to data centers now, 450 and 500 slated for 2027.Source:https://ai.meta.com/blog/meta-mtia-scale-ai-chips-for-billions/submitted by/u/Balance-[link][comments]
title_es: Meta acelera producción de chips MTIA propios para IA
bajada: Meta anunció cuatro generaciones de chips MTIA optimizados para inferencia, escalonando desde 300 hasta 500 con 4.5x más ancho de banda. Demuestra inversión agresiva en silicio personalizado para independencia de Nvidia.
bajada_en: Meta announced four generations of MTIA chips optimized for inference, scaling from 300 to 500 with 4.5x more bandwidth. Shows aggressive investment in custom silicon for Nvidia independence.

title: Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust
summary:
We built OneCLI because AI agents are being given raw API keys. And it's going about as well as you'd expect. We figured the answer isn't "don't give agents access," it's "give them access without giving them secrets."OneCLI is an open-source gateway that sits between your AI agents and the services they call. You store your real credentials once in OneCLI's encrypted vault, and give your agents placeholder keys. When an agent makes an HTTP call through the proxy, OneCLI matches the request by host/path, verifies the agent should have access, swaps the placeholder for the real credential, and forwards the request. The agent never touches the actual secret. It just uses CLI or MCP tools as normal.Try it in one line:
docker run --pull always -p 10254:10254 -p 10255:10255 -v onecli-data:/app/data ghcr.io/onecli/onecliThe proxy is written in Rust, the dashboard is Next.js, and secrets are AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest. Everything runs in a single Docker container with an embedded Postgres (PGlite), no external dependencies. Works with any agent framework (OpenClaw, NanoClaw, IronClaw, or anything that can set an HTTPS_PROXY).We started with what felt most urgent: agents shouldn't be holding raw credentials. 
The next layer is access policies and audit, defining what each agent can call, logging everything, and requiring human approval before sensitive actions go through.It's Apache-2.0 licensed. We'd love feedback on the approach, and we're especially curious how people are handling agent auth today.GitHub:https://github.com/onecli/onecliSite:https://onecli.shComments URL:https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47353558Points: 99# Comments: 36
title_es: OneCLI: bóveda segura para credenciales de agentes IA
bajada: OneCLI es un gateway open-source que protege credenciales API al darle agentes IA claves placeholder. Resuelve un problema crítico de seguridad: permitir que agentes tengan permisos sin exponer secretos reales.
bajada_en: OneCLI is an open-source gateway that protects API credentials by giving AI agents placeholder keys instead of real ones. Solves a critical security problem: letting agents have permissions without exposing actual secrets.

title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages

bajada_en: Sellers on Facebook Marketplace can use Meta AI to auto-draft buyer responses based on listing data. Expands AI use in Meta's apps beyond traditional chatbots.

title: Show HN: Axe – A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework
summary: I built Axe because I got tired of every AI tool trying to be a chatbot.Most frameworks want a long-lived session with a massive context window doing everything at once. That's expensive, slow, and fragile. Good software is small, focused, and composable... AI agents should be too.Axe treats LLM agents like Unix programs. Each agent is a TOML config with a focused job. Such as code reviewer, log analyzer, commit message writer. You can run them from the CLI, pipe data in, get results out. You can use pipes to chain them together. Or trigger from cron, git hooks, CI.What Axe is:- 12MB binary, two dependencies. no framework, no Python, no Docker (unless you want it)- Stdin piping, something like `git diff | axe run reviewer` just works- Sub-agent delegation. Where agents call other agents via tool use, depth-limited- Persistent memory. If you want, agents can remember across runs without you managing state- MCP support. Axe can connect any MCP server to your agents- Built-in tools. Such as web_search and url_fetch out of the box- Multi-provider. Bring what you love to use.. Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, or anything in models.dev format- Path-sandboxed file ops. Keeps agents locked to a working directoryWritten in Go. No daemon, no GUI.What would you automate first?Comments URL:https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47350516Points: 119# Comments: 85
title_es: Axe: framework minimalista para agentes IA en 12MB
bajada: Axe es un binario de 12MB que trata agentes IA como programas Unix: pequeños, enfocados y componibles. Soporta piping, múltiples proveedores LLM y MCP, desafiando la tendencia de frameworks monolíticos.
bajada_en: Axe is a 12MB binary that treats AI agents as Unix programs: small, focused, and composable. Supports piping, multiple LLM providers, and MCP, challenging the trend of monolithic frameworks.

title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
